items.title: Kitolano dompelt De Kuip in rouw met 3-4 in blessuretijd
items.summary: Shaqueel van Persie scoorde in de slotfase twee keer voor Feyenoord en dacht zijn club te behoeden voor een nederlaag, maar dat was buiten Joshua Kitolano gerekend.

items.title: FC Twente verdubbelt voorsprong na controversiële handsbal
items.summary: FC Twente kwam tegen Heracles Almelo via elf meter op een 0-2 voorsprong. Ricky van Wolfswinkel scoorde, maar wat ging daar precies aan vooraf?

items.title: Chaotic AFCON final an embarrassment to soccer
items.summary: Fan clashes, VAR controversies, missed 'Panenka' penalties, a walkout, Senegal's extra-time winner over Morocco - the AFCON final had it all. But not in a good way.

items.title: Martens vierde ontslagen Eredivisie-trainer van dit seizoen
items.summary: Na FC Twente, Heracles Almelo en Ajax heeft nu ook AZ ingegrepen in de technische staf door de trainer te ontslaan. Maarten Martens kwam zondagavond in een rijtje met Joseph Oosting, Bas Sibum en John Heitinga te staan .

items.title: Frank expected to take charge of Spurs against Dortmund
items.summary: Thomas Frank is expected to remain in charge of Tottenham for their Champions League fixture against Borussia Dortmund on Tuesday night.

items.title: Chaos at AFCON final after Senegal walk off
items.summary: The Africa Cup of Nations final went to extra time after finishing 0-0 in a chaotic end which saw Senegal players walk off the field when host Morocco was awarded a stoppage-time penalty.

items.title: Shaqueel Van Persie zet De Kuip in vuur en vlam met geweldige omhaal
items.summary: Shaqueel van Persie dacht Feyenoord te behoeden voor een nederlaag tegen Sparta. De zoon van trainer Robin van Persie maakte zijn eerste twee doelpunten in het shirt van de club, maar bleef teleurgesteld achter.

items.title: Aftocht Martens roemloos: 'Zit één handsbal tussen'
items.summary: AZ heeft besloten om Maarten Martens per direct te ontslaan en in de ZSM blikt hoofdredacteur Pieter Zwart kort terug op zijn functioneren bij de Alkmaarders.

items.title: 'Something special going on!' Patriots one game from Super Bowl
items.summary: New England Patriots owner Robert Kraft told Sky Sports NFL this weekend that he believed his team were 'back'. They are now one game from being 'back' in a Super Bowl.

items.title: Shaka Hislop: ‘It might take another 100 years to dismantle racism but we’ll get there’
items.summary: Former Newcastle goalkeeper opens up on the abuse he has received and using the platform footballers have to support an anti-racism charity It was a chance encounter that would ultimately help change countless lives for the better but, at the time, all Shaka Hislop wanted to do was escape. As the then Newcastle goalkeeper stood on a petrol station forecourt, filling his car on a dark November night in 1995 his overriding emotions were outrage and fear. Hislop was heading home after an evening out with his wife and young daughter when, with the fuel gauge edging towards the red zone, he pulled into a garage just across the road from St James’ Park. Continue reading...

items.title: Premier League: 10 talking points from the weekend’s action
items.summary: Manchester United jog memories, Nick Woltemade comes up short and there’s a tough crowd for Chelsea’s owners Top scorers: check out the latest top-flight standings Sir Jim Ratcliffe was present to see the best victory and performance of his two years of minority ownership. When Ratcliffe bought in, the public impression given was of a billionaire signing up to taste the magic for himself. Saturday, and beating Manchester City, was an undoubted revival act where Michael Carrick’s team played the football of yore. That will almost certainly be unsustainable in the medium term, since most opposition will not play City’s high-line, high-wire act. But in engaging their supporters with determination and aggression, United jogged memories. There was a time when just about every big game had Old Trafford rocking like this, when the opposition could not hear themselves think. Surely that was the myth and legend Ratcliffe wanted to be part of? Would that be possible in the new stadium the Ineos chief has plans for instead of Old Trafford? Tottenham’s recent experiences suggest otherwise. Would Liverpool’s owners cash out the Anfield experience? Surely not. items.summary: Chelsea tell Real Madrid they could sell Enzo Fernandez, Man Utd interested in Joao Gomes, and Nottingham Forest in talks for Lorenzo Lucca.

items.title: Latest scores from Australian Open with Brit Fearnley in action along with Swiatek
Carrick gave fans 'taste' of Ferguson era - Rooney
items.summary: Wayne Rooney says Man Utd's younger fans got a "taste of what it was like under Sir Alex Ferguson" in the win over Man City - and hopes it is a glimpse of what is to come under Michael Carrick.

items.title: De Jong slams arrogant ref's attitude in Barça loss
items.summary: Barcelona midfielder Frenkie de Jong attacked referee Jesús Gil Manzano's arrogant attitude after the LaLiga leader's 11-game winning run was ended by Real Sociedad on Sunday.

items.title: Pulisic back in XI as Milan win, keep Inter in sight
Arsenal top women's Money League; Chelsea 2nd
items.summary: Arsenal Women topped the revenue list for the first time, generating €25.6 million ($29.6m, £22.5m) as top ranked women's football clubs generated over €150m ($173.7m, £130m), according to Deloitte Football Money League.

items.title: Diaz will 'have nightmares' over 'Panenka' failure
items.summary: Brahim Diaz had the chance to win Afcon for Morocco, but his choice to try a Panenka ended up costing his side dearly as Senegal hit back to win the game in extra time.
